title: Protecting American Capital Act of 2025
plain_summary: The Protecting American Capital Act of 2025 requires the Treasury Secretary to write reports every year about Americans who invest money in Chinese companies. The reports must list which Americans are putting money into Chinese businesses and which Chinese companies are getting that money. The Treasury Secretary has to pay special attention to big investors and Chinese companies that have been punished with sanctions. This helps the government keep track of where American money is going in China.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Protecting American Capital Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Annual Report on United States Portfolio Investments in the People's Republic of China
sections.text:
The Treasury Secretary must create a yearly report about American investments in China. This report must be given to Congress no later than one year after this law is passed, and then every year after that. The report must cover portfolio investments, which are investments in stocks, bonds, and other financial securities. It must include investments that go through other countries before reaching China.

Each report must examine who is making these investments and who is receiving them. For American investors, the report must identify what types of people and organizations are investing, including state pension funds that manage retirement money for government workers. It must also identify any American investor that makes more than two percent of all such investments in a single year.

For Chinese companies receiving the investments, the report must look at different parts of China's economy, including the housing sector. It must identify any Chinese companies that are under United States sanctions but still receive American investments. It must also identify Chinese companies that receive more than one hundred million dollars from American investments.

The first report must cover the time period from January first, two thousand eight until the report is finished. Every report after that must cover the one year period before the report is submitted.

This section defines important terms. A Chinese entity means any company or organization that is set up under Chinese laws or is controlled by the Chinese government. A United States person means either an American citizen, a legal permanent resident of America, or any company or organization created under American laws, including foreign offices of American companies.
